2:Tdec,{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name":"What's the current APY for ETH/IFARM Camelot?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"ETH/IFARM Camelot is showing a 24-hour APY of -, with a 30-day average of -. Rates are variable and move with trading volume on the ETH/IFARM pair, the GRAIL emission schedule, and overall liquidity in the pool. The figures reflect the realised yield over the trailing window; they are not a forward guarantee."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How does the autocompounding work?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"The strategy holds an LP position in the ETH/IFARM pool on Camelot and periodically claims any GRAIL rewards that accrue. Those rewards are then converted in the proportions needed to add liquidity back into the same pool, increasing the size of the LP position held by the vault and the value of each holder's share. The process repeats automatically; holders are not required to claim, swap, or add liquidity themselves. Autocompounding events run when economically feasible, anywhere from hourly to several days apart, with gas costs socialised across all holders."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Can I withdraw at any time?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"There are no withdrawal periods or lockups. If the underlying pool holds enough liquidity to satisfy the request, exits are instant. During periods of low pool liquidity, withdrawal capacity can be limited until liquidity returns. See the risk page for details on how this works."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Where does the yield come from?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yield comes from two sources. First, trading fees on the ETH/IFARM pool on Camelot: every swap between the two assets pays a fee, a share of which accrues to liquidity providers. Second, GRAIL emissions distributed by Camelot to incentivise liquidity in the pool, which the strategy claims and adds back into the position. Both move with conditions: trading fees scale with volume, and emissions scale with the platform's emission schedule."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How stable has the APY been?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Over the last 30 days, this vault's APY has ranged from - to -, averaging -, with measured volatility of ±0.00%. The Strategy stability section above shows where this falls on the scale from very volatile to very consistent."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How much is currently in the vault?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"The vault currently holds $74K in TVL across 19 holders. The Historical statistics section above shows how this compares to the vault's 30-day range and lifetime peak."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What are the risks?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Like any onchain yield strategy, this vault is exposed to smart contract risk in both the Harvest contracts and the underlying Camelot pool, and protocol-specific risks of the assets it holds. Because the position holds both ETH and IFARM, the value of the position also moves with the relative price of the two assets in the pair: when the two prices diverge, the LP position is worth less than holding the two tokens separately would have been. This is commonly referred to as impermanent loss. GRAIL rewards partially offset this, but the offset is not guaranteed and depends on emission rates and the magnitude of price divergence. Harvest's core vault infrastructure was audited by Halborn in January 2025. Audits reduce but do not eliminate risk."}}]}0:{"rsc":["$","$1","c",{"children":[[[["$","script",null,{"type":"application/ld+json","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"{\"@context\":\"https://schema.org\",\"@type\":\"FinancialProduct\",\"name\":\"ETH Camelot\",\"url\":\"https://harvest.finance/eth-camelot-ifarm-arbitrum\",\"description\":\"ETH Camelot on Camelot (Arbitrum). Yield strategy indexed by Harvest.\",\"provider\":{\"@type\":\"Organization\",\"name\":\"Camelot\"},\"category\":\"Camelot - Arbitrum\",\"feesAndCommissionsSpecification\":\"N/A\"}"}}],["$","script",null,{"type":"application/ld+json","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"{\"@context\":\"https://schema.org\",\"@type\":\"BreadcrumbList\",\"itemListElement\":[{\"@type\":\"ListItem\",\"position\":1,\"name\":\"Home\",\"item\":\"https://harvest.finance\"},{\"@type\":\"ListItem\",\"position\":2,\"name\":\"ETH Ranking\",\"item\":\"https://harvest.finance/eth\"},{\"@type\":\"ListItem\",\"position\":3,\"name\":\"ETH/IFARM Camelot\"}]}"}}],["$","script",null,{"type":"application/ld+json","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"{\"@context\":\"https://schema.org\",\"@type\":\"Dataset\",\"name\":\"ETH Camelot historical APY, TVL and share-price data\",\"description\":\"Daily APY (622 points), TVL (170 points) and share-price (624 points) history for the ETH Camelot vault on Arbitrum, indexed by Harvest.\",\"url\":\"https://harvest.finance/eth-camelot-ifarm-arbitrum#history\",\"creator\":{\"@type\":\"Organization\",\"name\":\"Harvest\",\"url\":\"https://harvest.finance\"},\"temporalCoverage\":\"2023-05-29/2026-05-15\",\"keywords\":[\"ETH\",\"Arbitrum\",\"Harvest Finance\",\"Camelot - Arbitrum\",\"DeFi\",\"yield\",\"APY\",\"TVL\"],\"license\":\"https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/\",\"isAccessibleForFree\":true,\"distribution\":{\"@type\":\"DataDownload\",\"encodingFormat\":\"text/html\",\"contentUrl\":\"https://harvest.finance/eth-camelot-ifarm-arbitrum#history\"}}"}}]],["$","script",null,{"type":"application/ld+json","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$2"}}],"$L3"],["$L4","$L5"],"$L6"]}],"isPartial":false,"staleTime":300,"varyParams":null,"buildId":"q6wKIb0gKKN7A126oJllP"}

d:["$","div",null,{"className":"uni-below","children":[["$","section",null,{"className":"pp-section","id":"about","children":[["$","h2",null,{"children":["About ","ETH/IFARM Camelot"]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"about-prose","children":[["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"ETH/IFARM Camelot"}]," is an ","LP-token autocompounder on Arbitrum, with ETH paired with IFARM in the underlying LP position. The strategy provides liquidity to the ETH/IFARM pool on Camelot and earns yield from both trading fees on the pair and GRAIL emissions distributed to liquidity providers."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Any claimed GRAIL rewards are automatically converted into more of the underlying LP position and added back to the vault, removing the manual claim and conversion steps a user would otherwise need to perform on their own. Autocompounding events run when economically feasible, anywhere from hourly to several days apart, with gas costs socialised across all holders rather than borne by each user individually."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Live since May 2023. Currently indexed at $74K TVL across 19 holders."}]]}]]}],["$","section",null,{"className":"pp-section","id":"performance-overview","children":[["$","h2",null,{"children":"Performance Overview"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"pp-numbered-list","children":[["$","div","0",{"className":"pp-numbered-item","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"pp-num-badge","children":"01"}],["$","span",null,{"className":"pp-num-text","children":"Over the past 30 days, APY has ranged from - to -, averaging -. 23:["$","div",null,{"className":"pp-section","id":"faq","children":[["$","h2",null,{"children":"Frequently Asked Questions"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"faq","children":[["$","details","0",{"open":true,"children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"What's the current APY for ETH/IFARM Camelot?"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"ETH/IFARM Camelot is showing a 24-hour APY of -, with a 30-day average of -. Rates are variable and move with trading volume on the ETH/IFARM pair, the GRAIL emission schedule, and overall liquidity in the pool. The figures reflect the realised yield over the trailing window; they are not a forward guarantee."}]]}],["$","details","1",{"open":false,"children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"How does the autocompounding work?"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"The strategy holds an LP position in the ETH/IFARM pool on Camelot and periodically claims any GRAIL rewards that accrue. Those rewards are then converted in the proportions needed to add liquidity back into the same pool, increasing the size of the LP position held by the vault and the value of each holder's share. The process repeats automatically; holders are not required to claim, swap, or add liquidity themselves. Autocompounding events run when economically feasible, anywhere from hourly to several days apart, with gas costs socialised across all holders."}]]}],["$","details","2",{"open":false,"children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Can I withdraw at any time?"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["There are no withdrawal periods or lockups. If the underlying pool holds enough liquidity to satisfy the request, exits are instant. During periods of low pool liquidity, withdrawal capacity can be limited until liquidity returns. See ",["$","$L8",null,{"href":"/risk-framework","children":"the risk page"}]," for details on how this works."]}]]}],["$","details","3",{"open":false,"children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Where does the yield come from?"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Yield comes from two sources. First, trading fees on the ETH/IFARM pool on Camelot: every swap between the two assets pays a fee, a share of which accrues to liquidity providers. Second, GRAIL emissions distributed by Camelot to incentivise liquidity in the pool, which the strategy claims and adds back into the position. Both move with conditions: trading fees scale with volume, and emissions scale with the platform's emission schedule."}]]}],["$","details","4",{"open":false,"children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"How stable has the APY been?"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Over the last 30 days, this vault's APY has ranged from - to -, averaging -, with measured volatility of ±0.00%. The Strategy stability section above shows where this falls on the scale from very volatile to very consistent."}]]}],["$","details","5",{"open":false,"children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"How much is currently in the vault?"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"The vault currently holds $74K in TVL across 19 holders. The Historical statistics section above shows how this compares to the vault's 30-day range and lifetime peak."}]]}],["$","details","6",{"open":false,"children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"What are the risks?"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Like any onchain yield strategy, this vault is exposed to smart contract risk in both the Harvest contracts and the underlying Camelot pool, and protocol-specific risks of the assets it holds. Because the position holds both ETH and IFARM, the value of the position also moves with the relative price of the two assets in the pair: when the two prices diverge, the LP position is worth less than holding the two tokens separately would have been. This is commonly referred to as impermanent loss. GRAIL rewards partially offset this, but the offset is not guaranteed and depends on emission rates and the magnitude of price divergence. Harvest's core vault infrastructure was audited by Halborn in January 2025. Audits reduce but do not eliminate risk."}]]}]]}]]}]
